page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers of compute and memory capabilities. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1102.69, while multi-core performance reached 2935.19, both falling into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 164.26 GB/sec, but this drops significantly to a poor 20.87 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. Cryptographic and compression workloads also reflect these constraints, with single-threaded gzip compression at 19.26 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.88 GB/sec.

The instance is suited for lightweight workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but have minimal memory footprints. Example use cases include basic web serving, where it achieves a static web serving throughput of 3.47 GB/sec, or simple key-value operations, as demonstrated by a Redis SET performance of 2.88 million operations per second. It can also handle lightweight text processing and small-scale software compilation, though it is not designed for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 4.0 GB of system memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance consistently in the lower tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2935.19 and a large-block uncached memory read bandwidth of 20.87 GB/sec. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, this instance is best suited for lightweight, CPU-dedicated workloads with small memory footprints, such as basic static web serving, minor text processing, and low-volume Redis key-value operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight, compute-dedicated workloads.

tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual server with a minimal memory footprint, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.

4.0 GB of system memory providing 1.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
KVM hypervisor architecture on x86_64 platform
Best suited for basic web serving and lightweight key-value databases
